Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Decade of Slug 684 upvotes, 66 comments
  2. More than 135 open hardware devices flashable with your own firmware 294 upvotes, 36 comments
  3. A tale about fixing eBPF spinlock issues in the Linux kernel 127 upvotes, 13 comments
  4. Nightingale – open-source karaoke app that works with any song on your computer 279 upvotes, 42 comments
  5. Write up of my homebrew CPU build 154 upvotes, 22 comments
  6. Using calculus to do number theory 26 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. Unsloth Studio 347 upvotes, 65 comments
  8. Mistral AI Releases Forge 615 upvotes, 147 comments
  9. A Tiny Camera Revealed a Hidden Passage in the Great Pyramid 32 upvotes, 3 comments
  10. (Media over QUIC) on a Boat 72 upvotes, 12 comments
  11. JPEG Compression 327 upvotes, 81 comments
  12. Show HN: Sub-millisecond VM sandboxes using CoW memory forking 225 upvotes, 59 comments
  13. Pandas Exercises for Data Analysis (Interactive) 74 upvotes, 17 comments
  14. Microsoft's 'unhackable' Xbox One has been hacked by 'Bliss' 744 upvotes, 277 comments
  15. Celebrating Tony Hoare's mark on computer science 76 upvotes, 21 comments
  16. The pleasures of poor product design 187 upvotes, 64 comments
  17. It Took Me 30 Years to Solve This VFX Problem – Green Screen Problem [video] 272 upvotes, 104 comments
  18. Electron microscopy shows ‘mouse bite’ defects in semiconductors 94 upvotes, 30 comments
  19. How the Eon Team Produced a Virtual Embodied Fly 53 upvotes, 15 comments
  20. Show HN: Pgit – A Git-like CLI backed by PostgreSQL 88 upvotes, 32 comments
  21. Tech hobbyist makes shoulder-mounted guided missile prototype with $96 in parts 93 upvotes, 40 comments
  22. Get Shit Done: A meta-prompting, context engineering and spec-driven dev system 385 upvotes, 205 comments
  23. Have a fucking website 660 upvotes, 373 comments
  24. Ryugu asteroid samples contain all DNA and RNA building blocks 287 upvotes, 152 comments
  25. Rob Pike's 5 Rules of Programming 400 upvotes, 226 comments
  26. Python 3.15's JIT is now back on track 438 upvotes, 255 comments
  27. Federal Cyber Experts Called Microsoft's Cloud "A Pile of Shit", yet Approved It 29 upvotes, 10 comments
  28. Why AI systems don't learn – On autonomous learning from cognitive science 160 upvotes, 94 comments
  29. Hundreds of Millions of iPhones Can Be Hacked With a New Tool Found in the Wild 17 upvotes, 6 comments
  30. A ngrok-style secure tunnel server written in Rust and Open Source 4 upvotes, 1 comments

Last generated 11:00pm, 18 March 2026. Source here.
